news.title: Americans have become more likely to say Trump is not too or not at all religious
news.summary_en: Among Trump’s strongest supporters, relatively few people say they think he is very religious. Just 8% of Republicans and 5% of White evangelicals say this is the case.

news.title: Americans stand out internationally for their pessimism about the nation’s political system
news.summary_en: People in higher-income countries are generally less likely to say their nation’s political system needs major changes or complete reform. Americans are an exception.

news.title: Teens’ Experiences on TikTok, Instagram and Snapchat
news.summary_en: Teens largely turn to TikTok, Instagram and Snapchat for fun and connection. But experiences around messaging, screen time and cyberbullying vary. And what teens say about how these sites impact their mental health.

news.title: Multiple indicators show a decline in the health of America’s democracy in 2025
news.summary_en: Most Americans say democracy in the United States used to be a good example for other countries to follow but has not been in recent years.

news.title: Americans’ views of China have grown somewhat more positive in recent years
news.summary_en: Most Americans still view China negatively, but a recent survey shows favorability has ticked up, especially among Democrats and younger adults. Fewer now call China an enemy.

news.title: Local News Fact Sheet
news.summary_en: Americans’ attention to local news has declined since 2016. Explore how people get local news today, shifting from TV and print to digital sources.

news.title: Most new data centers in the U.S. are coming to rural areas
news.summary_en: Over 1,500 new data centers are in development nationwide. Most will be built in rural areas, particularly the South and Midwest, marking a shift from urban locations.
